content: I reorder the same groceries every week and barely open other categories. [ps#0]
Saved lists run my shopping. Autopilot reorder means zero browse intent. [ps#1]
Same basket every Sunday - milk, eggs, snacks. Routine never changes. [ps#2]
Once items are in my list I stop exploring. Habit locks me in. [ps#3]
Trust risk stops me from pet supplies - unsure about freshness and authenticity. [ps#4]
Opaque fees make me avoid trying new categories at checkout. [ps#5]
Personal care feels irrelevant to me; relevance doubt is real. [ps#6]
Would try baby products but price uncertainty and quality fear hold me back. [ps#7]
I discover products only through search. Homepage banners feel irrelevant. [ps#8]
Recommendations never show adjacent categories - always snacks again. [ps#9]
Friend told me about household essentials; word of mouth beats the app. [ps#10]
Social proof from deep reviews mattered more than any in-app cue. [ps#11]
Need ingredients and use-case guidance before trying personal care. [ps#12]
Want clearer returns and freshness info before a new category trial. [ps#13]
Review depth is too thin for categories I have never bought. [ps#14]
Sizing and pack freshness cues are missing on unfamiliar SKUs. [ps#15]
Frequent stockouts push me back to my usual basket. [ps#16]
Late delivery twice - not experimenting when ETA slips. [ps#17]
Poor substitutes kill willingness to try unfamiliar items. [ps#18]
Support is unreachable; I stick to categories I already trust. [ps#19]
Tried personal care only because of a big discount. Deals drive my trials. [ps#20]
After we got a puppy I finally opened pet supplies - life event trigger. [ps#21]
Festival sale made me buy gift hampers outside my usual categories. [ps#22]
Niche diet interest is the only reason I browse beyond groceries. [ps#23]
No starter kits for new categories. Hard to know what to buy first. [ps#24]
Assortment feels thin compared to specialty stores. [ps#25]
Cross-category cues are weak; app never nudges adjacent aisles. [ps#26]
Weak guidance for first-time buyers in baby and pet. [ps#27]
